description: You use the elements in the art folder to manage adapter components, including connections, adapter services, listeners, and notifications.

services.name: pub.art:listRegisteredAdapters
description: WmART. Returns the display name and adapter type name of all registered adapters.

output_parameters.name: registeredAdapterList
description: Information for each adapter registered with the WmART package.

children.name: adapterDisplayName
description: The localized name that the Integration Server Administrator displays.

children.name: adapterTypeName
description: The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package. This value can be used as input for the inventory services that take adapterTypeName as input.

services.name: pub.art.connection:disableConnection
description: WmART. Disables a connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
description: Name of the connection node you want to disable.

services.name: pub.art.connection:enableConnection
description: WmART. Enables an existing connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
description: Name of the connection node you want to enable.

services.name: pub.art.connection:getConnectionStatistics
description: WmART. Returns current usage statistics for a connection node.

input_parameters.name: aliasName
description: Name of the connection node for which you want usage statistics returned.

output_parameters.name: connectionStatistics
description: Information for each connection node.

children.name: TotalConnections
description: Current number of connection instances.

children.name: BusyConnections
description: Number of connections currently in use by services, notifications, and listeners.

children.name: FreeConnections
description: Total number of connections created and available for use.

children.name: TotalHits
description: Number of times this connection node successfully provided connections since the last reset.

children.name: TotalMisses
description: Number of times this connection node unsuccessfully provided connections since the last reset (when the request timed out).

services.name: pub.art.connection:listAdapterConnections
description: WmART. Lists connection nodes associated with a specified adapter.

input_parameters.name: adapterTypeName
description: The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package.

output_parameters.name: connectionDataList
description: Information for each connection node registered with the specified adapter.

children.name: connectionAlias
description: The name of the connection node.

children.name: packageName
description: The name of the package in which the connection node resides.

children.name: connectionState
description: - disabled - Connection node is disabled
- enabled - Connection node is enabled.
- shuttingdown - Connection node is in the process of shutting down.
- unknown - Connection node is registered but has not yet established its state.

services.name: pub.art.connection:queryConnectionState
description: WmART. Returns the current connection state (enabled/disabled) and error status for a connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
description: Name of the connection node for which you want the connection state and error status returned.

output_parameters.name: connectionState
description: Current connection state (enabled/disabled).

output_parameters.name: hasError
description: - true if an error was detected.
- false if no error was detected.

services.name: pub.art.listener:disableListener
description: WmART. Disables a listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
description: Name of the listener you want to disable. The listener should have a state of enabled or suspended.

input_parameters.name: forceDisable
description: - true to disable the listener.
- false to keep the listener enabled.

services.name: pub.art.listener:enableListener
description: WmART. Enables an existing listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
description: Name of the listener you want to enable.

usage_notes: If you do not enable the connection resource associated with the listener, this service will return without performing any action, and the listener will remain disabled. Therefore, you should invoke pub.art.listener:queryListenerState before calling this service to confirm that the listener has been enabled.

services.name: pub.art.listener:listAdapterListeners
description: WmART. Lists listeners associated with a specified adapter.

input_parameters.name: adapterTypeName
description: The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package.

output_parameters.name: listenerDataList
description: Information for each listener registered with the specified adapter.

children.name: listenerNodeName
description: The name of the listener.

children.name: packageName
description: The name of the package in which the listener resides.

children.name: listenerEnabled
description: - disabled if the listener is disabled.
- enabled if the listener is enabled.
- enablePending if the listener is in the process of starting.
- disablePending if the listener is in the process of disabling.
- suspended if the listener is suspended.
- suspendPending if the listener is in the process of suspending.

services.name: pub.art.listener:queryListenerState
description: WmART. Returns the current state for a listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
description: Name of the listener for which you want the current state returned.

output_parameters.name: listenerState
description: - disabled if the listener is disabled.
- enabled if the listener is enabled.
- enablePending if the listener is in the process of starting.
- disablePending if the listener is in the process of disabling.
- suspended if the listener is suspended.
- suspendPending if the listener is in the process of suspending.

services.name: pub.art.listener:resumeListener
description: WmART. Resumes a specified listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
description: The name of the suspended listener you want to resume. The service returns an error if you specify an invalid listener.

usage_notes: If the requested transition is not valid (for example, trying to resume a disabled listener or a listener that is already resumed), the service ignores the request.
After you use this service, you can use pub.art.listener:queryListenerState to verify pub.art.listener:resumeListener correctly changed the state of the listener.

services.name: pub.art.listener:setListenerNodeConnection
description: WmART. Changes the connection node used by a specified listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
description: Name of the listener for which you want to change the connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
description: Name of the new connection node to use with the listener.

usage_notes: Calling this service for a listener that is disabled is permitted.
Calling this service for a listener that is suspended changes the state of the listener to disabled. The user must enable the listener before using it.

services.name: pub.art.listener:suspendListener
description: WmART. Suspends a specified listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
description: The name of the listener you want to suspend. The service returns an error if you specify an invalid listener.

usage_notes: If the requested transition is not valid (for example, trying to suspend a disabled listener or a listener that is already suspended), the service ignores the request.
After you use this service, you can use pub.art.listener:queryListenerState to verify pub.art.listener:suspendListener correctly changed the state of the listener.

services.name: pub.art.notification:disableListenerNotification
description: WmART. Disables a listener notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: The name of the listener notification you want to disable.

services.name: pub.art.notification:disablePollingNotification
description: WmART. Disables a polling notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: The name of the polling notification you want to disable. The polling notification should have a state of enabled or suspended.

services.name: pub.art.notification:disablePublishEvents
description: WmART. Disables the publishing of events for an adapter notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: The name of the notification for which you want to disable the publishing of events.

services.name: pub.art.notification:enableListenerNotification
description: WmART. Enables an existing listener notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: The name of the listener notification you want to enable.

services.name: pub.art.notification:enablePollingNotification
description: WmART. Enables an existing polling notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: Name of the polling notification you want to enable.

usage_notes: You must schedule the polling notification before you can run this service. See your adapter user documentation for instructions to schedule the polling notification.

services.name: pub.art.notification:enablePublishEvents
description: WmART. Enables the publishing of events for an adapter notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: The name of the notification for which you want to enable the publishing of events.

services.name: pub.art.notification:listAdapterListenerNotifications
description: WmART. Lists the listener notifications associated with a specified adapter.

input_parameters.name: adapterTypeName
description: The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package.

output_parameters.name: notificationDataList
description: Information for each listener notification registered with the specified adapter.

children.name: notificationNodeName
description: The name of the listener notification.

children.name: packageName
description: The name of the package in which the listener notification resides.

children.name: notificationEnabled
description: - no if the listener notification is disabled.
- yes if the listener notification is enabled.

services.name: pub.art.notification:listAdapterPollingNotifications
description: WmART. Lists the polling notifications associated with a specified adapter.

input_parameters.name: adapterTypeName
description: The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package.

output_parameters.name: notificationDataList
description: Information for each polling notification registered with the specified adapter.

children.name: notificationNodeName
description: The name of the polling notification.

children.name: packageName
description: The name of the package in which the polling notification resides.

children.name: notificationEnabled
description: - no if the polling notification is disabled.
- yes if the polling notification is enabled.
- pending if the polling notification is in the process of shutting down.
- suspended if the polling notification is suspended.

services.name: pub.art.notification:queryListenerNotificationState
description: WmART. Returns the current state (enabled/disabled) for a listener notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: The name of the listener notification for which you want the current state (enabled/disabled) returned.

output_parameters.name: notificationState
description: The current state (enabled/disabled) for the listener notification.

services.name: pub.art.notification:queryPollingNotificationState
description: WmART. Returns the current state for a polling notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: The name of the polling notification for which you want the current state and schedule settings returned.

output_parameters.name: notificationState
description: The current state (enabled, disabled, pending disable, pending suspend, or suspended) for the polling notification.

output_parameters.name: scheduleSettings
description: Object that contains the notification's schedule settings as follows:

children.name: notificationInterval
description: Polling frequency of the notification.

children.name: notificationOverlap
description: - true if the notification can overlap.
- false if the notification cannot overlap.

children.name: notificationImmediate
description: - true if the notification can fire immediately.
- false if the notification cannot fire immediately.

services.name: pub.art.notification:resumePollingNotification
description: WmART. Resumes a specified polling notification node.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: The name of the polling notification you want to resume. The service returns an error if you specify an invalid polling notification.

usage_notes: If the requested transition is not valid (for example, trying to resume a disabled polling notification or a polling notification that is already resumed), the service ignores the request.

After you use this service, you can use pub.art.notification:queryPollingNotificationState to verify pub.art.notification:resumePollingNotification correctly changed the state of the polling notification to enabled.

services.name: pub.art.notification:setListenerNotificationNodeListener
description: WmART. Changes the listener used by a specified listener notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: Name of the listener notification for which you want to change the listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerNode
description: Name of the new listener to use with the listener notification.

usage_notes: This service returns an error if the listener notification is enabled.
You can use this service for synchronous and asynchronous listener notifications.

services.name: pub.art.notification:setPollingNotificationNodeConnection
description: WmART. Changes the connection node used by a specified polling notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: Name of the polling notification for which you want to change the connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
description: Name of the new connection node to use with the polling notification.

usage_notes: The polling notification must be in a disabled or suspended state before you call this service. This service returns an error if the polling notification is enabled.
If you use this service on a suspended polling notification, the service changes the state of the polling notification to disabled.

services.name: pub.art.notification:suspendPollingNotification
description: WmART. Suspends a specified polling notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
description: The name of the polling notification you want to suspend. The service returns an error if you specify an invalid polling notification.

usage_notes: If the requested transition is not valid (for example, trying to suspend a disabled polling notification or a polling notification that is already suspended), the service ignores the request.

After you use this service, you can use pub.art.notification:queryPollingNotificationState to verify pub.art.notification:suspendPollingNotification correctly changed the state of the polling notification to suspended.

services.name: pub.art.service:listAdapterServices
description: WmART. Lists adapter services associated with a specified adapter.

input_parameters.name: adapterTypeName
description: The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package.

output_parameters.name: serviceDataList
description: Information for each adapter service registered with the specified adapter.

children.name: serviceNodeName
description: The name of the adapter service.

children.name: packageName
description: The name of the package in which the adapter service resides.

services.name: pub.art.service:setAdapterServiceNodeConnection
description: WmART. Changes the connection node used by a specified adapter service.

input_parameters.name: serviceName
description: Name of an existing adapter service for which you want to change the connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
description: Name of the new connection node to use with the adapter service.

usage_notes: The new connection node must be enabled before you call this service.

services.name: pub.art.transaction:commitTransaction
description: WmART. Commits an explicit transaction.

input_parameters.name: commitTransactionInput
description: Information for each commit request.

children.name: transactionName
description: This value must be mapped from the most recent pub.art.transaction:startTransaction that has not previously been committed or rolled back.

usage_notes: This service is available only if your adapter supports built-in transaction management services, which you can confirm by checking the user guide for the adapter.

This service must be used in conjunction with the pub.art.transaction:startTransaction service. If the transactionName parameter was not provided in a prior call to pub.art.transaction:startTransaction, a run-time error will be returned.

services.name: pub.art.transaction:rollbackTransaction
description: WmART. Rolls back an explicit transaction.

input_parameters.name: rollbackTransactionInput
description: Information for each rollback request.

children.name: transactionName
description: This value must be mapped from the most recent pub.art.transaction:startTransaction that has not previously been committed or rolled back.

usage_notes: This service is available only if your adapter supports built-in transaction management services, which you can confirm by checking the adapter's user guide.

This service must be used in conjunction with the pub.art.transaction:startTransaction service. If the given transactionName parameter was not provided in a prior call to pub.art.transaction:startTransaction, a run-time error will be returned.

services.name: pub.art.transaction:setTransactionTimeout
description: WmART. Manually sets a transaction timeout interval for implicit and explicit transactions.

input_parameters.name: timeoutSeconds
description: The number of seconds that the implicit or explicit transaction stays open before the transaction manager marks it for rollback.

usage_notes: This service is available only if your adapter supports built-in transaction management services, which you can confirm by checking the user guide for the adapter.
When you use this service, you are temporarily overriding the Integration Server transaction timeout interval.

You must call this service within a flow before the start of any implicit or explicit transactions. Implicit transactions start when you call an adapter service in a flow. Explicit transactions start when you call the pub.art.transaction:startTransaction service.
If the execution of a transaction takes longer than the transaction timeout interval, all transacted operations are rolled back.
This service only overrides the transaction timeout interval for the flow service in which you call it.

services.name: pub.art.transaction:startTransaction
description: WmART. Starts an explicit transaction.

input_parameters.name: startTransactionInput
description: Information for each start transaction request.

children.name: transactionName
description: Optional. Specifies the name of the transaction to be started. If you leave this parameter blank, Integration Server will generate a name for you. In most implementations it is not necessary to provide your own transaction name.

output_parameters.name: startTransactionOutput
description: Information for each start transaction request.

children.name: transactionName
description: The name of the transaction the service just started.

usage_notes: This service is available only if your adapter supports built-in transaction management services, which you can confirm by checking the user guide for the adapter.

This service is intended for use with the pub.art.transaction:commitTransaction or pub.art.transaction:rollbackTransaction service. The transactionName value returned by a call to this service can be provided to pub.art.transaction:commitTransaction (to commit the transaction) or pub.art.transaction:rollbackTransaction (to roll back the transaction).
